result.claim: The CoQA dataset was used in this study.
result.original_text: Using the CoQA dataset

result.claim: The CoQA dataset contains 7199 dialogues.
result.original_text: The dataset contains 7199 dialogues

result.claim: The dataset contains dialogues with different numbers of questions each.
result.original_text: with different number of questions each

result.claim: Questions in the dataset are interconnected and often rely on context from previous dialogue turns.
result.original_text: Questions are interconnected, often relying on context from previous turns in the dialogue

result.claim: The study analyzes a subset of dialogues containing exactly 20 questions.
result.original_text: this study specifically analyzes the subset of dialogues containing exactly 20 questions

result.claim: 1000 dialogues were considered in the simulation.
result.original_text: 1000 dialogues were considered.

result.claim: For approaches 1 and 3, 100 identical dialogues with 20 questions were considered.
result.original_text: For approaches 1 and 3, 100 identical dialogues with 20 questions were considered.

result.claim: For the second approach, a total of 85 dialogues were used.
result.original_text: For the second approach, a total of 85 dialogues were used.

result.claim: The Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 language model was used in this study.
result.original_text: the Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 language model

result.claim: The first 60% of questions (12 questions) are used as prompt questions.
result.original_text: the first 60% of questions (in this case, 12) will be used to feed the model

result.claim: The remaining 40% of questions (8 questions) are used as test/evaluate questions.
result.original_text: the remaining 40% (8) will be used later as test questions

result.claim: The total number of EQs per dialogue (M) is 8.
result.original_text: M is a total EQ per dialogue (8 in our case).

result.claim: The topic of the dialogue was extracted using an LLM.
result.original_text: the topic of the dialogue was extracted using LLM.

result.claim: DBSCAN was used to group dialogue topics into clusters.
result.original_text: using DBSCAN (Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise), the topics of the dialogues were grouped into clusters.

result.claim: For the second approach, dialogues were grouped into 20 clusters.
result.original_text: grouped into 20 clusters.

result.claim: The LLM-as-a-judge comparison uses a scale from 0 to 1.
result.original_text: The comparison uses a scale from 0 to 1

result.claim: A score of 0.5 is defined as 'Possibly equivalent (similar intent but different phrasing)'.
result.original_text: where 0.5 is "Possibly equivalent (similar intent but different phrasing)"

result.claim: Question pairs with a similarity score no less than 0.5 are considered similar.
result.original_text: pairs of questions that yield a result no less than this threshold are considered similar

result.claim: The Expected Value (EV) metric is calculated as the average percentage of test questions per dialogue that can be predicted.
result.original_text: This metric represents the average percentage of test questions per dialogue that can be predicted.

result.claim: The topic-and-aspect-driven first approach achieved an expected value of 3.1% for the percentage of successfully predicted test questions.
result.original_text: the topic-andaspect-driven first approach (EV=3,1%)

result.claim: The multi-dialogue cluster-based approach achieved an expected value of 2.4% for the percentage of successfully predicted test questions.
result.original_text: the multi-dialogue cluster-based approach (EV=2,4%)

result.claim: The direct context-based approach achieved an expected value of 2.4% for the percentage of successfully predicted test questions.
result.original_text: the direct context-based approach (EV=2,4%)
